item.title: Pavements obstructed, 7th June
item.description:

item.title: Crashed car abandoned, 7th June
item.description:

item.title: Vegetation overhanging narrow pavememt, 7th June
item.description:
Hedges and Lavender bushes overhanging the walls of a narrow pavement reducing the width. On a walking route to Farrington’s school and Chislehurst School for Girls.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Perry Street Gardens, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish, 7th June
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to make a formal complaint regarding the ongoing misuse of the communal bins on our estate by people who do not live here. As a leaseholder, I pay service charges for the maintenance, cleaning and upkeep of the estate. However, despite these charges, residents are constantly forced to live with overflowing bins, rubbish dumped on the ground and non-residents using our bin facilities as if they were public waste disposal points. Today I witnessed yet another vehicle unloading rubbish into the estate bins. I have photographs of the vehicle and its registration number, which I am attaching. The situation has become unacceptable. Residents have to walk past this area every day to access their homes. The bin area is frequently surrounded by rubbish, discarded items and waste left on the ground. At times it is difficult to pass through comfortably and safely. Quite frankly, it is disgusting that residents who pay service charges are expected to put up with these conditions while outsiders continue to use the estate as a dumping ground. I would like to know what action the council intends to take to address this ongoing problem and prevent non-residents from using facilities that are paid for by estate residents. I look forward to your response. Kind regards,

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): McNeil Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Manhole open, 7th June
item.description:
Broken/missing

item.title: Street light damaged on Lawn Close, 7th June
item.description:
The street light casing near ground level has been damaged. There is ugly tape around it saying 'CAUTION ELECTRIC CABLE', but it has not been fixed in over three months. Please could it be fixed ASAP.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lawn Close, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
